conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules so I can ask you the right questions.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. I've reviewed the eligibility rules for Child Benefit. To help determine if you're eligible, I'll need to ask you some questions.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have two children - Alex is 5 and Blake is 10. They both live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. It's helpful to know that Alex (5) and Blake (10) both live with you.

Do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't. I'm living abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for letting me know. Which country are you currently living in?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. And just to confirm - are Alex and Blake living with you in Spain, or are they living in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: They're both living with me here in Spain.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that you and both children are living in Spain.

Are you a Crown servant (such as a diplomat or member of HM Forces serving abroad)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Let me ask a few more questions to fully assess your situation.

Do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. 

Are you working for a UK employer or paying UK National Insurance contributions while living in Spain?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not working for a UK employer or paying UK National Insurance contributions.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for answering all my questions. I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex and Blake.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I'm sorry to inform you that **you are not eligible for Child Benefit** for either Alex or Blake.

**For Alex (age 5):** While Alex is under 16 and lives with you, the key issue is that you do not live in the UK. The fundamental requirement for Child Benefit is that you must live in the UK. Since you're living in Spain and are not a Crown servant or in any other exceptional circumstance that would allow you to claim while abroad, you do not qualify for Child Benefit for Alex.

**For Blake (age 10):** The same applies to Blake. Although Blake is under 16 and lives with you, you do not meet the UK residency requirement. Without being a Crown servant or having another qualifying arrangement, you cannot claim Child Benefit for Blake while living in Spain.

The eligibility rules make it clear that you normally qualify for Child Benefit only if you're responsible for a child under 16 **and** you live in the UK. While there are limited exceptions for people living abroad (such as Crown servants), you do not fall into any of these exception categories.

If your circumstances change in the future - for example, if you move back to the UK - you would be able to apply for Child Benefit at that time.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex (age 5) does not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 AND you live in the UK. While Alex is under 16 and lives with you (meeting the age and responsibility requirements), you do not live in the UK - you live in Spain. The exceptions to the UK residency requirement include being a Crown servant or living in certain countries with specific arrangements, but you are not a Crown servant and have not indicated any special circumstances that would allow you to claim while living in Spain. Therefore, Alex does not qualify.

Blake (age 10) does not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 AND you live in the UK. While Blake is under 16 and lives with you (meeting the age and responsibility requirements), you do not live in the UK - you live in Spain. The exceptions to the UK residency requirement include being a Crown servant or living in certain countries with specific arrangements, but you are not a Crown servant and have not indicated any special circumstances that would allow you to claim while living in Spain. Therefore, Blake does not qualify.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is responsible for two children, Alex (5) and Blake (10), both of whom live with them. However, the claimant and both children currently live in Spain, not the UK. The fundamental eligibility requirement for Child Benefit is that the claimant must live in the UK. While there are exceptions for Crown servants or certain specific circumstances, the claimant does not meet any of these exceptions. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for either child.
